name: DEFECT/DAMAGED PACKAGE-CLEARANCE: Dream Bentonite Clumping Cat Litter Odourless 10l
shortDescription: 100% natural bentonite clumping cat litter for happy and clean cats. Instantly lumps , thereby preventing bacteria and odour from taking over your cat's litter box.
description: Keep your feline friend happy and your home clean with Proline Bentonite Clumping Cat Litter. Made from 100% natural bentonite, this litter quickly forms solid clumps upon contact with moisture, effectively trapping bacteria and odour before they can spread. Key Features: Odourless Formula: Neutralizes Odour without heavy perfumes. Enjoy a fresh environment! Superior Clumping Action: Instantly forms tight clumps for easy scooping and minimal mess, Excellent pelletising, Instantly lumps. Natural Ingredients: Non-toxic and 100% natural Safe for your pets and the planet, made from pure bentonite clay. Dust-Free: Helps reduce tracking and maintains a cleaner home. Prevents bacterial growth Give your cat the comfort they deserve with Proline Bentonite Cat Litter – the ideal choice for a happy, clean litter box! Using cat litter for kittens and adult cats involves a few key steps to ensure a clean and comfortable experience for your pets. Here’s a guide to help you: Choosing the Right Litter Type of Litter : There are various types of cat litter, including clumping clay, non-clumping clay, biodegradable, and crystal litter. For kittens, non-clumping or biodegradable options are often recommended to avoid ingestion issues. Scent : Some litters are scented, but unscented options may be better for sensitive cats or kittens. Setting Up the Litter Box Box Size : Choose a litter box that’s appropriate for your cat's size. Kittens may need a smaller box at first, but they’ll grow quickly. Location : Place the litter box in a quiet, accessible area away from food and water bowls. Avoid high-traffic areas. Depth : Fill the box with about 2-3 inches of litter. This is generally enough for cats to dig and cover their waste. Training Kittens and Cats Introducing the Box : Show your kitten or cat where the litter box is. You can gently place them in the box after meals or naps, as they often need to relieve themselves at those times. Positive Reinforcement : Reward your cat with treats or praise when they use the litter box correctly. This helps reinforce good behavior. Maintenance Scooping : Scoop the litter box daily to remove waste and clumps. This helps keep the box clean and odor-free. Changing Litter : Change the litter entirely at least once a week, or more often if you have multiple cats. Clean the box with mild soap and water before refilling. Monitoring : Keep an eye on your cat’s litter box habits. Changes in frequency or appearance of stool can indicate health issues. Additional Tips Multiple Boxes : If you have more than one cat, provide one litter box per cat plus one extra to avoid territorial disputes. Litter Training : If your kitten isn’t using the litter box, check for stressors or litter preferences, and consult a vet if necessary. By following these guidelines, you can create a comfortable and clean environment for your feline friends! Upon first use, we recommend adding new silica cat litter to the surface of the old cat litter for a few days
